name: OIDC resilience and conformance validation
outcome: The completed OIDC surface is validated against cross-cutting failure modes and a real relying-party client before the release docs are finalized.
scope: Add cross-cutting negative e2e coverage and an openid-client smoke/conformance checklist spanning JWKS, discovery, ID token verification, UserInfo, and token-confusion defenses. No new feature behavior beyond tightening tests and documenting any discovered gaps.
acceptance_criteria: A negative e2e suite proves algorithm-confusion, alg:none, wrong typ, wrong issuer, expired token, revoked token, missing openid scope, and opaque-code nonce-loss cases fail with the pinned status/error responses from the feature issues
JWKS and discovery cache headers are asserted end-to-end so relying-party caching behavior is not accidentally changed
The keystone e2e uses real timers and jose verification, not JwtService self-verification
An openid-client smoke recipe or script runs against the example/vanilla server and records pass/fail output suitable for the release checklist
Any gaps found by the smoke run are either fixed in the owning feature issue or recorded in prd.json risks/open_questions before issue 9 finalizes docs
steps: test/e2e OIDC negative suite — compose completed endpoints from issues 7 and 8 and assert the cross-cutting invalid_token/insufficient_scope/invalid_grant cases listed in acceptance criteria
test/e2e JWKS/discovery headers — verify Cache-Control and Content-Type through the public AuthorizationServer endpoint methods rather than just builders
test/e2e keystone audit — ensure the existing keystone path uses vi.useRealTimers() and jose verification against the live jwks() response
smoke script or docs fixture — add a copy-pasteable openid-client flow against the vanilla/example server and record expected output
prd.json risks/open_questions — update only if the smoke/conformance run uncovers a real limitation or release-blocking ambiguity
notes: Added during PRD review because the original plan embedded high-risk failure tests across feature issues but lacked a dedicated cross-cutting resilience gate. This issue runs after UserInfo and discovery exist and blocks final docs/release integration.
